h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can copy that order now.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue confirming the four-part label arrangement and proceed to copy it into the packet.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets the lab packet line under the tray edge. 'The bottom line matches 12.01, so that space gets atomic mass. Top 6 is atomic number, C is symbol, carbon is name, and 12.01 stays separate. I will copy them into the matching spaces.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically aligns the packet to the tray edge and articulates the one-to-one mapping to answer spaces, which is a modest enacted step beyond mere verbal confirmation, though it stops short of actually completing the copy task on the page.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established scaffolding route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same spatial-separation device across all four turns without introducing any new representation, role, evidence standard, or task.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and all tutor turns simply continue the pre-established plan.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically aligns the packet to the tray edge and articulates the one-to-one mapping to answer spaces, which is a modest enacted step beyond mere verbal confirmation, though it stops short of actually completing the copy task on the page.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and all tutor turns simply continue the pre-established plan.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established scaffolding route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established scaffolding route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same spatial-separation device across all four turns without introducing any new representation, role, evidence standard, or task.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: That names the interaction on the cart, not the cart's motion.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue applying the 'object on the cart' formula to each remaining arrow in sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Table on the cart, for the friction contact. The arrow direction checks which force it is, but the label is the object acting on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner completes the labeling task and articulates the criterion (arrow direction identifies the force; label names the acting object), showing the device has been internalized, though the articulation closely mirrors the tutor's scaffolding rather than constituting a genuinely novel intellectual move.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction; the tutor continues the same 'object on the cart' scaffolding route throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same labeling scaffold across all three tutor turns without introducing a changed device, representation, or evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor uptake to a revised learner frame; the tutor simply continues the pre-established routine.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction; the tutor continues the same 'object on the cart' scaffolding route throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction; the tutor continues the same 'object on the cart' scaffolding route throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same labeling scaffold across all three tutor turns without introducing a changed device, representation, or evidence standard.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a straight trajectory of consolidating and repeating the two-label formula, and every subsequent learner turn continues exactly that path.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [draws the bar line where the acetate strip sits.]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 performs a concrete physical action—drawing the bar line—that enacts the spatial criterion rather than merely restating it, though the verbal content remains the same formula repeated throughout.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and no adaptive mechanism is deployed; the tutor simply reinforces the same route throughout.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same labeling-and-confirmation move in every turn without introducing a new device, representation, or evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; all tutor turns follow the pre-established consolidation script.
